As is known, the multipath error on a pseudo-range is larger than the measuring
noise. Therefore, the CMCR can be used to characterize multipath errors. Shown in
Figures5and6arethestatisticsRMSvaluesofmultipatherrorsfortheseobservedGPS
andBDSsatellitesinboththestaticanddynamicexperiments. Accordingly,inthestatic

4.2. PerformanceofthePresentedSmartphoneDynamicPPP
Inthissection,theresultsofthepresentedsmartphonePPPbasedontheBDSPPP-B2b
products and IGS real-time products in the dynamic test are presented. The reference
results for this dynamic experiment are calculated by the tight RTK/INS integration.
ThedynamicsmartphoneŌĆÖsGPS+BDSdataareprocessedby(1)GPS-onlyPPPusingthe
BDS PPP-B2b products based on the hybrid weight function and the mixed-frequency
measurementsforL1andL5(GPS-PPP-b2b-MF-Com);(2)GPS+BDS-3PPPusingtheBDS
